title: DocuLink WebView2 Message Protocol v1
description: Defines all messages exchanged between the C# host and the web apps over the WebView2 postMessage channel.

ViewerReadyMessage.description: Sent once by the document-viewer web app after JavaScript has initialised. The host responds by posting a PdfsLoadedMessage.

PdfPayload.description: A single PDF document embedded in the workbook.

id.description: GUID that uniquely identifies this PDF within the workbook.

PdfsLoadedMessage.description: Sent by the C# host to push the current workbook's PDF list to the viewer. Re-sent after any add/remove operation.

PdfUpdatedMessage.description: Sent by the C# host when a single PDF's bytes change (e.g. after OCR). The viewer should update its entry list and reload the document if it is currently displayed.

ManagerReadyMessage.description: Sent once by the file-manager web app after JavaScript has initialised. The host responds by posting a FilesLoadedMessage.

FolderPayload.description: A named folder used to organise PDF documents.

id.description: GUID that uniquely identifies this folder.

FileMetadataPayload.description: Metadata for a single PDF in the file manager. Does NOT include base64 bytes.

id.description: GUID matching the storage Pdf id.

folderId.description: GUID of the owning folder, or omitted if uncategorised.

dateAdded.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime when the file was added.

FilesLoadedMessage.description: Sent by the C# host to push the current workbook's file list to the file manager. Re-sent after any mutation.

AddFilePayload.description: A single file to be added, as sent from the web UI.

AddFilesMessage.description: Sent by the file-manager web app when the user drops or picks files.

RenameFileMessage.description: Sent by the file-manager web app when the user renames a file.

RemoveFileMessage.description: Sent by the file-manager web app when the user removes a file.

MoveFileMessage.description: Sent by the file-manager web app when the user moves a file to a different folder. Send folderId as null/omitted to move to uncategorised.

AddFolderMessage.description: Sent by the file-manager web app when the user creates a new folder.

RenameFolderMessage.description: Sent by the file-manager web app when the user renames a folder.

RemoveFolderMessage.description: Sent by the file-manager web app when the user deletes a folder. Files in the folder become uncategorised.

SetSelectedFolderMessage.description: Sent when the user changes the active folder in the file manager. Used by the host when importing files from OS drag/drop (Explorer) so new PDFs match the current selection. Omit folderId when 'All Files' is selected.

folderId.description: GUID of the selected folder, absent for uncategorised / All Files.

RectPayload.description: A normalized rectangle (0–1 coordinate space, top-left origin) on a single PDF page.

LinkRectangleCreatedMessage.description: Sent by the document-viewer when the user finishes drawing a link rectangle. Contains the PDF region and the extracted, formatted text ready for cell insertion.

pdfId.description: GUID of the PDF the rectangle was drawn on.

text.description: Extracted and formatted text contained within the rectangle.

CacheBuildStartedMessage.description: Sent by the document-viewer immediately before building the per-page text-content cache for the loaded PDF. The host should show a loading indicator.

CacheBuildCompleteMessage.description: Sent by the document-viewer once the text-content cache has been fully built. The host should hide the loading indicator.

LinkedRectanglePayload.description: A single persisted link rectangle, as sent from the C# host to the viewer.

id.description: GUID that uniquely identifies this linked rectangle.

pdfId.description: GUID of the PDF this rectangle belongs to.

LinkedRectanglesLoadedMessage.description: Sent by the C# host to push the full set of persisted link rectangles to the viewer. Re-sent after any add operation so the viewer stays in sync.

LinkRectangleClickedMessage.description: Sent by the document-viewer when the user clicks a persisted link rectangle overlay. The host responds by activating the linked worksheet and selecting the linked cell.

id.description: GUID of the LinkedRectangle that was clicked.

NavigateToRectangleMessage.description: Sent by the C# host when the user selects a linked cell in Excel. The viewer should switch to the specified PDF (if needed), jump instantly to the page, and highlight the rectangle.

id.description: GUID of the LinkedRectangle to highlight.

pdfId.description: GUID of the PDF containing the rectangle.

ClearRectangleHighlightMessage.description: Sent by the C# host when the user selects a cell that is not linked to any rectangle. The viewer should remove any active rectangle highlight.

OcrPdfsMessage.description: Sent by the file-manager web app when the user clicks 'OCR Selected'. The host runs OCR on each PDF and replies with one or more OcrStatusMessages.

pdfIds.description: GUIDs of the PDFs to OCR.

OcrStatusMessage.description: Sent by the C# host to the file-manager to report per-PDF OCR progress. One message is sent for each status transition: queued → processing → complete | error.

pdfId.description: GUID of the PDF being processed.

status.description: Current OCR lifecycle state for this PDF.

message.description: Optional human-readable detail, typically populated on error.
